summary: Set 047 Session 2 shipped the reader-first phase of the v4 schema migration: the normalize_to_v4_shape / normalizeToV4Shape shim in Python and TypeScript, reader routing in fileSystem.ts (readSessionSets pipes raw JSON through the shim; v2-compat ledger merge hoisted above the normalize call; needsMigration reads rawSd not normalized sd), 32 Python + 31 TS unit tests covering v3-in / v4-in / v2-in / errors / pure-function guarantee / routing / verifier-flagged regressions / idempotence, and a readSessionSets() perf benchmark with baseline mean=21.8ms p50=21.2ms p95=32.5ms max=32.5ms over 47 sets × 20 iterations. Cross-provider verification ran two rounds against gpt-5-4 tier 3 ($0.484 total): Round 1 flagged 3 must-fix items, Round 2 confirmed Issues 1 and 2 resolved. Issue 3 (expanding the needsMigration detector to flag raw v3 files) was deferred to Session 3 with a documented code-comment rationale — the dabblerSessionSets.migrate command is hardwired to "Migrate to v3 schema" and flipping the flag now would point the badge CTA at a nonexistent v3→v4 migrator (which ships in Session 3). All 850 Python tests pass + 11 e2e + 8 e2e-marker; 563 TS unit tests pass (2 pre-existing failures unchanged). Cumulative S1+S2 routed cost: $0.59202 of $10 NTE (5.9%).

reason.specifics: Set 047 Session 3 is the migrator phase per the scope-locked spec.md §4: ships `python -m ai_router.migrate_v3_to_v4` CLI subcommand (walks docs/session-sets/*/session-state.json, idempotent re-runs, dry-run + apply modes, writes session-state.v3.bak.json alongside on apply), the TypeScript Explorer right-click 'Migrate to v4 schema' command, the formal rollback procedure doc deliverable, a new Layer-3 Playwright spec for the right-click migrate path, AND the deferred-from-S2 needsMigration detector expansion (raw v3 files now flag as needing v4 migration, with the badge CTA pointing at the new v4 migrator). Claude Opus 4.7 high-effort is the same orchestrator that closed S1 and S2 and is appropriate for the cross-language migrator authoring (Python CLI + TS command) plus the Playwright spec authoring.
